name: Java Collections Framework (JCF) Deep Dive
description:
The Java Collections Framework (JCF)

The JCF is a unified architecture for representing and manipulating collections. It allows collections to be manipulated independently of the details of their representation.

  • Hierarchy: Rooted at Iterable and Collection for data structures, with Map as a separate hierarchy for key-value pairs.
  • List: Ordered collections that allow duplicates (ArrayList, LinkedList).
  • Set: Unordered collections that prohibit duplicates (HashSet, TreeSet).
  • Map: Key-Value mappings (HashMap, TreeMap).
  • Queue/Deque: Designed for holding elements prior to processing (PriorityQueue, ArrayDeque).

This visualization breaks down the relationships between core interfaces and their concrete implementations, distinguishing between standard modern collections and legacy components.
code:
graph TD
  subgraph Root_Interfaces [Root Interfaces]
    Iterable[Iterable] 
    Collection[Collection]
  end

  subgraph List_Hierarchy [List Type]
    ListI[List Interface]
    ArrayList[ArrayList]
    LinkedList[LinkedList]
    Vector[Vector]
    Stack[Stack]
  end

  subgraph Set_Hierarchy [Set Type]
    SetI[Set Interface]
    SortedSetI[SortedSet Interface]
    NavigableSetI[NavigableSet Interface]
    HashSet[HashSet]
    LinkedHashSet[LinkedHashSet]
    TreeSet[TreeSet]
  end

  subgraph Queue_Hierarchy [Queue & Deque Type]
    QueueI[Queue Interface]
    DequeI[Deque Interface]
    PriorityQueue[PriorityQueue]
    ArrayDeque[ArrayDeque]
  end

  subgraph Map_Hierarchy [Map Hierarchy]
    MapI[Map Interface]
    SortedMapI[SortedMap Interface]
    NavigableMapI[NavigableMap Interface]
    HashMap[HashMap]
    LinkedHashMap[LinkedHashMap]
    TreeMap[TreeMap]
    Hashtable[Hashtable]
  end

  %% Relationships
  Iterable --> Collection
  Collection --> ListI
  Collection --> SetI
  Collection --> QueueI

  %% List Flow
  ListI --> ArrayList
  ListI --> LinkedList
  ListI --> Vector
  Vector --> Stack

  %% Set Flow
  SetI --> HashSet
  SetI --> SortedSetI
  HashSet --> LinkedHashSet
  SortedSetI --> NavigableSetI
  NavigableSetI --> TreeSet

  %% Queue Flow
  QueueI --> DequeI
  QueueI --> PriorityQueue
  DequeI --> ArrayDeque
  DequeI --> LinkedList

  %% Map Flow
  MapI --> HashMap
  MapI --> Hashtable
  MapI --> SortedMapI
  HashMap --> LinkedHashMap
  SortedMapI --> NavigableMapI
  NavigableMapI --> TreeMap

  %% Styling Subgraphs
  style Root_Interfaces fill:transparent,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style List_Hierarchy fill:transparent,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style Set_Hierarchy fill:transparent,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style Queue_Hierarchy fill:transparent,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style Map_Hierarchy fill:transparent,stroke:#94a3b8,stroke-dasharray: 5 5

  %% Class Definitions
  classDef interface fill:#e0f2fe,stroke:#0369a1,color:#000,font-weight:bold
  classDef concrete fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#15803d,color:#000
  classDef legacy fill:#fff7ed,stroke:#c2410c,color:#000

  class Iterable,Collection,ListI,SetI,SortedSetI,NavigableSetI,QueueI,DequeI,MapI,SortedMapI,NavigableMapI interface
  class ArrayList,LinkedList,HashSet,LinkedHashSet,TreeSet,PriorityQueue,ArrayDeque,HashMap,LinkedHashMap,TreeMap concrete
  class Vector,Stack,Hashtable legacy
